qa_pairs.question: What is the primary benefit of dynamic workload balancing when using META mode for high-throughput computing?
qa_pairs.answer: When employing META mode for high-throughput computing, dynamic workload balancing ensures that all metajobs finish around the same time. This occurs irrespective of variations in individual case runtimes, CPU speeds across different nodes, or the specific start times of individual metajobs.

qa_pairs.question: If a metajob fails (e.g., due to a node crash) while running a farm in META mode, what is the impact on the cases being processed, and how can any lost work be recovered?
qa_pairs.answer: If a metajob dies (e.g., due to a node crash) in META mode, at most one case will be lost. This lost work can be easily rectified by using the `resubmit.run` command.
